lesson.menu_desc: Persuasive Language in Supplier Negotiations -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Making Your Case -- Persuasive Language in Supplier Negotiations
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Fabiana Michelly Silva | Aula 6 -- Making Your Case

listenings.text: Hi Fabiana, this is Klaus. Before the negotiation tomorrow, I want to be transparent about where TechFlow stands. What we are looking for is a longer contract, and it is the commitment that matters most to us. We can offer a volume discount, but a rebate on past orders is not on the table. It is the rising tariffs that have pushed our costs up, so there is little room on the unit price. What I would suggest is a trade-off: if you accept a two-year term, we can improve the value proposition with faster fulfillment and priority support. Our bottom line is firm, but we want a win-win. If we reach a deadlock on price, let us find common ground on the terms instead. Talk tomorrow.

Hi team, here is a quick recap of the negotiation with TechFlow. It went better than expected. What we secured is a five percent volume discount and faster fulfillment, and it is the longer term that unlocked it. They would not move on a rebate, so we made a trade-off: we accepted a two-year contract, and in return they strengthened their value proposition with priority support. It was the tariff issue that kept the unit price firm, but we held our bottom line on payment terms. At one point we hit a deadlock, and what saved the deal was finding common ground on delivery dates. Overall it is a win-win, and the incentive of a long partnership convinced them. I will send the summary shortly. Let me know if anything needs to be added.

extra_audio.text: Klaus leaves a voicemail explaining where TechFlow stands. Fabiana opens the negotiation and presents her value proposition. They reach a deadlock on the unit price because of new tariffs. A trade-off is agreed: a longer term in exchange for a volume discount. Both sides close a win-win deal, and the incentive of a long partnership seals it.
